title: Hybrid Mobile App Developer for Fitness Startup

client.name: Repsetta
client.signals: Early-stage startup building a mobile-first fitness platform. Seeking long-term partner for hybrid development. Fitness domain expertise valued but not required; engineering depth and proven hybrid framework experience are priorities. Budget-conscious (avg $93.75/hr against $50-85 posted range) and actively interviewing.

summary: Repsetta is hiring a long-term hybrid mobile app developer (React Native or Flutter) to build high-quality fitness applications. They value both experience and value, working <30 hrs/week over 6+ months. The role requires shipping to both iOS and Android from a single codebase, with a strong emphasis on user-friendly UX in the fitness domain.

explicit_requirements.text: Experience in Health and Wellness domain
Experience with React Native and/or Flutter (hybrid frameworks)
Able to commit for 6+ months (long-term engagement)
Located in Las Vegas Area or Twin Cities (US-only)
Answer proposal question: What is your experience in Health and Wellness?
Answer proposal question: What interests you with this project?
Answer proposal question: Describe your recent experience with similar projects
Answer proposal question: Please list any certifications related to this project
Answer proposal question: What frameworks have you worked with?
Ability to build high-quality, user-friendly applications

implicit_needs: Ship single codebase to both iOS and Android (cross-platform deployment)
Backend API integration capability (likely REST/GraphQL endpoints for fitness data)
Fitness domain product sense: workout tracking, progress metrics, user engagement patterns
Clean, modern UX design sensibility for fitness app context
Ability to work autonomously in a long-term, part-time capacity
Communication skills (noted preference for Conversational English)
tech_hints: Backend API integration (REST or GraphQL implied)
success_signals: Live, working React Native fitness app demonstrating core features (workout tracking, progress display, user authentication)
Evidence of shipping to both iOS and Android from a single codebase
Clean, intuitive UI that shows fitness domain understanding
Backend integration working smoothly (mocked or real)
Proof of long-term project engagement and reliability
Rate alignment with their stated $50-85/hr budget (they are paying $93.75 avg against that range)
timeline: 6+ months, <30 hours per week, long-term engagement
red_flags: HARD LOCATION GATE: 'MUST BE LOCATED IN LAS VEGAS AREA OR TWIN CITIES.' Michael did not confirm he is in either location; the cover letter must address this honestly and lead with engineering depth + live demo instead of fabricating location.
US-only applicants; location preference narrowed to Central/Pacific Time Zones + Minnesota/Nevada.
Competitive marketplace: only <5 proposals but 31 invites sent, 2 already interviewing. Pricing pressure evident (avg bid $93.75 vs. posted range $50-85).
No explicit mention of backend requirements or API specs; will need to infer from fitness domain during research.

demo_concepts.name: Workout Logger with Set/Rep Tracking and Rest Timer
demo_concepts.what: A React Native fitness app where users log exercises, track sets and reps, and follow guided rest timers between sets.
demo_concepts.hero_feature: Real-time rest timer with visual countdown and audio cue; tap to log sets and watch reps auto-increment with weight/difficulty picker.
demo_concepts.why_wins: Demonstrates core fitness UX (input, feedback, progress capture) and hybrid cross-platform capability. Showcases Tailwind styling and real backend integration. Immediate, tangible value to a fitness startup.

demo_concepts.name: Guided Workout Program Player
demo_concepts.what: A structured workout player that displays exercises from a program, shows form cues and video previews, and tracks completion.
demo_concepts.hero_feature: Full-screen workout view with exercise cards, step-by-step instructions, and one-tap 'complete' marking with progress persistence.
demo_concepts.why_wins: Illustrates product-thinking in fitness (exercise sequencing, user guidance). Shows ability to manage complex app state (current exercise, completed sets, program progress). Strong for a startup building a workout experience.

demo_concepts.name: Progress Dashboard with Charts and Stats
demo_concepts.what: A dashboard showing workout history, total volume (weight x reps), personal records, and week-over-week trends via simple charts.
demo_concepts.hero_feature: Interactive line chart showing progression over time; card layout showing key stats (PR lifts, total workouts, streak). Real data from backend.
demo_concepts.why_wins: Demonstrates ability to consume and visualize fitness data. Shows data-driven product thinking. Appeals to users who want to see measurable progress. Exercices both React Native + backend query patterns.

notes_from_michael: Build an ACTUAL React Native app using NativeWind/Tailwind for styling. Use mw-backend as the demo backend (runs on Michael's Surface). Claude must NOTIFY Michael when pushing to mw-backend so he can restart the server. Until then, simulate the backend or run a local Python server on the Mac, then append mw-backend functionality and re-test. Research Repsetta to infer what their app likely needs and bake those use cases into the build. The app must be web-exportable (Expo + react-native-web) so it can embed in the /work-samples area. Location gate (Vegas/Twin Cities, US-only): do NOT fabricate a location; acknowledge it honestly and lead with engineering depth + live demo. Certifications: answer honestly (no formal fitness certs assumed); emphasize app/engineering depth and fitness-domain fit.
